title: polygala supina subsp. hospita
intro: Polygala supina subsp. hospita, also known as the creeping polygala, is a species of flowering plant in the milkwort family (Polygalaceae). It is used in traditional medicine, particularly in China, where it is valued for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Polygala supina subsp. hospita are said to include the treatment of conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to support immune function and reduce stress. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used in tea, tinctures, or decoctions, and may be combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
